text:
Once upon a time in a small town called Evergreen, there was an old owl named Ottway. Ottway spent his days observing wolves. He learned how they hunted. He knew death.

One day, hunting season began. Wolves became hungry. Ottway saw them take down a young elk. He stayed silent. The wolves vanished.

Later that night, Ottway slept alone. He dreamed a wolf. It chased him. He fell.

When he woke, snow. Maybe elk. He stumbled.

Days. Birds sang. Owls quiet.

He knew.

He just knew.

Now it's harvest season.

A snipe. He shot.

Snow flew.

Ottway never saw it.

Perhaps wolves.

That's why he hunted. That's why ever.

Ottway knew death.

And he wondered.

What might live?

A girl.

Her feathers gray.

Blackberries on her jeans.

She saw him.

Maybe owl. Maybe boy.

He smiled.

She flew.

Stars watched.
